claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: You would initially think electrons are the same as photons because electrons are particles.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: Electrons can be measured, quantified, and counted individually as they go by, according to physicists.

claims.subject: double slit experiment
claims.claim: The double slit experiment is the number one most confounding, Twilight Zone-y thing ever.

claims.subject: protons
claims.claim: When protons are shot through a single slit, they fill up the other side in exactly the shape of the slit, as expected.

claims.subject: protons
claims.claim: When protons are shot through two slits, they produce exactly the two-band pattern you would expect on the other side.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: When electrons are shot through a single slit, they behave like particles just like protons and produce the expected single-band pattern.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: When electrons are shot through two slits one at a time, individually in single file, they produce a wave interference pattern on the other side rather than two bands.

claims.subject: wave interference pattern
claims.claim: A wave interference pattern consists of a bright center line, gradually dimming toward the edges, then darkness, then another bright line, repeating — the result of waves canceling each other out.

claims.subject: double slit experiment
claims.claim: It is deeply confusing how electrons fired one at a time could cancel each other out to create a wave pattern, since there is no other electron for them to bump into.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He does not know exactly how the double slit experiment was physically conducted and speculates light from a bulb might cause particles to bounce off each other on the way through.

claims.subject: protons
claims.claim: Protons behave differently from electrons in the double slit experiment.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: The electron, unlike the proton, travels through the universe along filaments of a higher-dimensional wave pattern rather than a straight path.

claims.subject: universe
claims.claim: Everything emanates giant bubbles out into eternity, and these bubbles create a giant three-dimensional filament structure in a higher dimension.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: An unobserved electron does not travel along its own individual path but randomly follows the general filament paths of whatever electrons travel through.

claims.subject: double slit experiment
claims.claim: The wave behavior of single electrons in the double slit experiment would prove the existence of a higher-dimensional ether.

claims.subject: double slit experiment
claims.claim: When scientists attached a camera to watch the trajectory of each individual electron, the electrons stopped producing a wave pattern and instead behaved as ordinary particles.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: When observed, an electron goes through one specific slit and lands on the other side in the expected particle location, every time.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: Something about the laser used to detect the electron changed the electron's own behavior.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: When being watched, an electron behaves as though it knows it is observed and travels as a straight particle the whole way.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: When not being watched, an electron randomly follows the filament matrix, zigzagging and jumping between parallel universe trajectories.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes nobody has yet explained or even seriously attempted to explain how an electron knows it is being watched.

claims.subject: electrons
claims.claim: The laser beam hitting the electron should not be enough to make it become a particle, because electrons travel through space encountering all kinds of other particles all day long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes that human attention — the soul — actually changes the physical universe, as evidenced by the observer effect in the double slit experiment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He posits that when a person dies, their vibration changes and they move into a higher-frequency dimension.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In a higher-frequency dimension after death, a consciousness would float freely through the filament matrix and be more in touch with it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: At even higher vibrational levels, a consciousness could potentially gain control not just over electrons but over the behavior of protons and neutrons as well.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He explicitly acknowledges he is not smart enough to explain how the electron knows it is being watched.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He poses two open questions he wants viewers to answer: how electrons act as waves when fired one at a time, and how an electron knows it is being watched and why it then becomes a particle.
